untranslatable_reason: This signal requires order flow microstructure analysis (detecting counterparty order direction, participant-level trade sequencing, and order size relative to individual participant history). These are NOT available in the allowed indicators list, which contains only price, volume, and technical indicators. The core logic requires: (1) tracking individual participant order history across multiple trades, (2) detecting repeated same-side orders from a specific counterparty, (3) comparing order sizes to participant-specific baselines (not market volume), and (4) anticipating order placement based on behavioral patterns. None of these can be expressed using close, volume, sma_*, rsi_*, atr_*, or other market-level indicators. This is a market microstructure strategy requiring order book and participant-level data.
translation_notes: Signal MICROSTRUCTURE_DRY_11_1 is fundamentally based on observing order flow patterns of individual market participants (e.g., 'Jon tends to buy after he has bought'). The backtest engine provided only supports technical indicators derived from OHLCV bars. To implement this strategy, you would need: (1) order-level data with counterparty/participant IDs, (2) historical tracking of each participant's trade side and size, (3) custom logic to detect consecutive same-side trades within rolling windows indexed by participant, and (4) state management across bars to track 'time since last order from this participant'. Consider using a specialized order flow analysis platform or custom data connector that provides participant-level order history.
